questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education does a broker applicant need?
questions.explanation: A Mississippi broker applicant must complete 120 hours of approved pre-license education. Additionally, the applicant must have at least 12 months of active experience as a licensed salesperson.

questions.question_text: A salesperson wants to upgrade to a broker license. How much active salesperson experience is required?
questions.explanation: Mississippi requires a broker applicant to have at least 12 months of active experience as a licensed salesperson before applying for a broker license, in addition to completing 120 hours of pre-license education.

questions.question_text: A new licensee completes 20 hours of post-license education in her first year. What happens to her license?
questions.explanation: New licensees must complete 30 hours of post-license education (24 core + 6 elective) within the first 12 months. Completing only 20 hours means the requirement is unmet, resulting in license suspension.

questions.question_text: How are the 30 required post-license hours divided?
questions.explanation: Mississippi requires 30 hours of post-license education within the first 12 months: 24 hours of core curriculum and 6 hours of elective courses. Failure to complete these hours results in license suspension.

questions.question_text: A new salesperson obtained her license in January. By when must she finish post-license education?
questions.explanation: Post-license education must be completed within the first 12 months of obtaining a license. For a January licensee, this means by the following January. Failure results in suspension.

questions.question_text: How many total hours of continuing education must a licensee complete each biennium?
questions.explanation: Mississippi requires 16 hours of continuing education per biennium: 4 hours agency law, 2 hours contract law, 2 hours license law, and 8 hours of electives.

questions.question_text: Of the 16 biennial CE hours, how many must cover agency law?
questions.explanation: The 16-hour biennial CE requirement includes 4 hours of agency law, 2 hours of contract law, 2 hours of license law, and 8 hours of elective courses.

questions.question_text: A 67-year-old broker has held a Mississippi license for 30 years. Is she exempt from continuing education?
distractors: No, all licensees must complete CE
Only if she is inactive status
Only if MREC approves a waiver
questions.explanation: Mississippi provides a senior CE exemption for licensees who are age 65 or older AND have been licensed for 25 or more years. At 67 with 30 years licensed, she meets both criteria.

questions.question_text: A 66-year-old licensee has been licensed for 20 years. Does she qualify for the senior CE exemption?
questions.explanation: The senior CE exemption requires BOTH age 65+ AND 25+ years of licensure. While she meets the age requirement at 66, she has only 20 years of licensure, falling short of the 25-year requirement.

questions.question_text: What minimum score must a licensee achieve on CE course exams?
questions.explanation: Mississippi requires licensees to score at least 70% on continuing education course exams to receive credit. This ensures licensees actually learn the required material rather than just attending courses.

questions.question_text: A licensee places her license on inactive status. Can she continue to practice real estate?
distractors: Yes, but for existing clients only
questions.explanation: A licensee with an inactive license cannot engage in any real estate activities that require a license. The license must be reactivated before the licensee can resume practicing.

questions.question_text: Under SB 2086, when do updated license fee changes take effect?
questions.explanation: SB 2086 (2025) updated the fee structure for Mississippi real estate licenses, with the changes becoming effective July 2025. This legislation also addressed exam retake fees and other licensing provisions.

questions.question_text: Under 2025 rules, how much notice must MREC give before suspending a license?
questions.explanation: As of 2025, MREC must provide at least 5 business days of email notice before making changes to a licensee's status, including suspension, revocation, or non-renewal.

questions.question_text: A licensee's renewal deadline passes without renewal. What is the license status?
questions.explanation: Failure to renew a license by the deadline results in the license becoming inactive or lapsed. The licensee cannot practice until the license is properly renewed or reactivated through MREC's procedures.

questions.question_text: MREC plans to revoke a licensee's license. Under 2025 rules, what must MREC do first?
questions.explanation: Under 2025 rules, MREC must provide at least 5 business days of email notice before making any license status change, including revocation. This requirement applies to suspension, revocation, and non-renewal actions.
